fact: May 20 is exactly 140 days into the year - that's exactly 20 weeks of perfect calendar math!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for fireflies to start their glow-up season and begin flashing their lights at each other!
The sun usually rises before 6 AM in most northern cities by May 20, which means even sleepyheads get blasted with morning light.
Dandelions are often going wild right now in northern climates - a single plant can produce up to 2,000 seeds that float away like tiny parachutes.
We're only about a month away from the summer solstice, so days are getting longer by about 2 minutes every single day in northern places.
May 20 typically falls in the middle of Taurus season - the zodiac sign of the stubborn bull.
In many temperate regions, this is when morning dew gets so heavy it looks like someone sprayed the grass with a giant water bottle overnight.
Migrating hummingbirds are usually zooming back to northern gardens right around now, beating their wings 80 times per second like tiny helicopters.
When there's a full moon on May 20, it's called the Flower Moon because everything is typically blooming like crazy in the Northern Hemisphere.
In the Southern Hemisphere, May 20 is deep into autumn, so leaves are turning colors and falling while northern kids are getting ready for summer.
Tree pollen is often at its peak around this time in northern climates - one oak tree can dump 28 pounds of yellow dust that makes everyone sneeze.
May 20 is exactly 225 days before New Year's Day, which means we're past the halfway point of the year's countdown.
